serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot black coffee, approximately 240 ml (240 g), as typically served in a café or restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central — SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry FDC ID 171890 for plain brewed black coffee prepared with tap water. This is an exact match for hot brewed black coffee with no added milk, sugar, or cream. Input per_100g values from GrabFood (a forbidden source) have been superseded by USDA SR Legacy values for approved-source compliance.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Database — beverages with negligible carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black coffee contains 0 g digestible carbohydrate per 100 g per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI value to beverages with negligible carbohydrate content. GI is recorded as 0 (sentinel for not_applicable in this schema) and gi_category is set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per 240 g serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content is 0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg/100 g); moderate caffeine intake may cause transient blood pressure elevation in sensitive individuals
hypertension.guidance: Plain black coffee is very low in sodium (2 mg/100 g) and is suitable for hypertensive individuals in moderate amounts of 1–2 cups per day; excessive caffeine intake exceeding 300 mg per day may cause acute transient blood pressure elevation in caffeine-sensitive individuals and should be avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero digestible carbohydrates and zero sugar; no glycaemic impact; chlorogenic acids associated with improved insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened black coffee contains no digestible carbohydrates or sugar and has no glycaemic impact; epidemiological evidence associates regular moderate consumption with improved insulin sensitivity, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes provided no sugar, syrups, or sweetened creamers are added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0 g/100 g), zero trans fat, very low sodium; polyphenols and chlorogenic acids may support vascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain black coffee contains no saturated fat, no trans fat, and negligible sodium, and moderate consumption of 3–5 cups per day has been associated in meta-analyses with neutral to modestly beneficial cardiovascular outcomes; individuals with arrhythmias or severe hypertension should limit caffeine intake to 1–2 cups per day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chlorogenic acids and polyphenols with documented anti-inflammatory properties; zero saturated fat and zero trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain black coffee provides chlorogenic acids and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties relevant to RA management; however, excessive caffeine may interfere with calcium absorption, so intake should be kept moderate at 1–2 cups per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Coffee (Black) is a plain brewed beverage made by passing hot water through ground roasted coffee beans, yielding a dark, aromatic liquid with no added milk, sugar, or cream. It is one of the most widely consumed beverages globally and is served hot, typically in a standard cup of approximately 240 ml. The flavour profile ranges from mildly bitter to richly complex depending on the roast level, origin of the beans, and brewing method (drip, pour-over, French press, or espresso-style). Nutritionally, plain black coffee is extremely low in calories (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein (0.12 g/100 ml), minimal fat (0.02 g/100 ml from coffee bean oils), and zero digestible carbohydrates or sugar, making it essentially calorie-free in standard serving sizes. It is naturally rich in caffeine (approximately 40 mg per 100 ml brewed) and contains chlorogenic acids, polyphenols, and B vitamins including riboflavin and niacin. Clinically, unsweetened black coffee has no glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when consumed in moderation. It is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, and kosher. The input record described this as a Fair Trade hot coffee from a GrabFood listing; nutritional values have been grounded in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for brewed black coffee (FDC ID 171890).
common_user_queries: How many calories in black coffee?
Is black coffee good for diabetes?
Does black coffee raise blood pressure?
How much caffeine in a cup of black coffee?
Is black coffee suitable for hypertension?
What is the glycemic index of black coffee?
Can I drink black coffee if I have cardiovascular disease?

confidence_reason: Nutritional values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (FDC ID 171890), which is an exact match for plain hot brewed coffee with no additives. Input GrabFood values were broadly consistent and have been superseded by the approved primary source.
notes: Fat_g is 0.02 g/100 g — trace amount naturally present in brewed coffee from coffee bean oils; not zero by formulation but negligible. Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890 for plain brewed black coffee — naturally zero as the brewing process does not extract significant digestible carbohydrates from coffee grounds. Sugar_g is naturally zero. Saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed coffee liquid. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible digestible carbohydrates (0 g/100 g per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. Input cultural metadata (primary_cuisine: Japanese, ethnic_tags: Japanese/Beef Dish/Japanese Curry/Grilled Chicken Skewers) was clearly erroneous for a generic hot coffee product and has been corrected to Global/Universal. No Japanese-language naming is required as this is not a Japanese dish. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.01), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.0), trans_fat_g (0.0), cholesterol_mg (0.0), vitamin_c_mg (0.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (0.0), vitamin_b6_mg (0.0), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.0), vitamin_e_mg (0.0), selenium_mcg (0.0) reported as confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890 for brewed black coffee. potassium_mg (49.0 mg), calcium_mg (2.0 mg), iron_mg (0.01 mg), phosphorus_mg (3.0 mg), magnesium_mg (3.0 mg), zinc_mg (0.02 mg), folate_mcg_dfe (2.0 mcg), water_g (99.39 g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890. Forbidden source (GrabFood) from input record has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the approved primary nutrient source.
